action.reasoning: Observation: Just achieved new best IC=0.04689 with the 3-seed CSRankNorm config (slightly better than iter-8's 0.0462). icir=0.3365, rank_ic=0.0553. Analysis: I have 14 iters and 3.47h left. The simple config beats more complex variants. Let me try one more variation: ensemble this 3-seed model with a slightly different config (e.g., add 2 more seeds with different num_leaves) by averaging predictions. Or try with more careful tuning. Actually safest path: try ensembling with linear model (Ridge on Alpha158) which is known to give good IC and has different errors than GBDT. Actually, let me try simple: increase to 5 seeds with same exact config to see if more seeds of same config helps. Decision: Try 5 seeds same config to see if averaging more reduces variance. Keep current best safe.
